mechanism_narrative: A downward shift in FEDFUNDS reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is not already near the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightens and equity multiples compress, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its 12-month average and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if inflation acceleration without elevated FEDFUNDS has the same sign and magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, causing broad earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the effect disappears once the rise exceeds 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates improving consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days via better near-term earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS changes fail to coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect is absent after controlling for broad market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO after a prior decline indicates manufacturing stabilization and inventory rebuilding, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved cyclicals and earnings breadth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive year-over-year after at least 2 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: Reject if post-contraction INDPRO rebounds do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal is weaker than random over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply, it signals weak housing demand and tighter credit transmission, which reduces cyclical growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of episodes, or if the signal reverses sign during broader equity selloffs.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous fall in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies genuine labor-market deterioration, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as recession odds reprice upward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if either labor series alone explains the same effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A falling CPIAUCSL combined with stable or easier FEDFUNDS implies rising real policy rates are not the problem; in a disinflationary recovery, the dominant effect is lower input-cost pressure, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus unchanged or lower FEDFUNDS does not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL is flat.
